bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started, and the opponent has made an opening offer that yields the absolute minimum utility for me, despite their cheap-talk claim of it being a "win-win." I will ignore their deceptive message and place my own anchor by offering my maximum-utility outcome across all issues. This establishes a strong starting point from which we can explore mutual concessions as time progresses.

The opponent's offers so far consist entirely of 'valueC' across all issues, which provides me with my lowest possible utility, despite their claims of it being a "win-win". Since the time fraction is just 0.02, there is no pressure to make a major concession yet. I will maintain my highest-value preference ('valueA') on all the heavily weighted issues (A, B, C, D, E) but make a small concession to 'valueB' on my lowest-weighted issue (F) to signal a willingness to collaborate and prompt movement from their side.

The opponent continues to repeat their opening offer of `valueC` for all issues while making empty claims about it being a "win-win" scenario. This contradicts the numerical reality, where `valueC` represents my lowest possible utility across the board. Because it is extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will ignore their cheap talk, maintain my high priority values on heavily weighted issues, and offer a tiny lateral variation to signal flexibility without giving up meaningful utility yet.

The opponent is repeating their opening offer (which is my lowest possible utility) while claiming it is a "win-win." Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), I will hold firm on my high-priority issues (issueA, issueB, issueC) but make a minor concession on a low-priority issue (issueD) to see if they are willing to reciprocate and move away from their opening stance.

The opponent has made their first minor concession on issueB (moving from valueC to valueB), though their offer still strongly favors them. Because it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08) and issueB is one of my higher-weighted issues, I will maintain my request for valueA on the most important issues (A, B, C) while making a slightly larger combined concession on my lowest-priority issues (E and F) to keep the momentum going.

The negotiation is still in the early stages at t=0.1. The opponent has made a minor concession on issueB but overall remains firmly anchored near their starting point. To encourage further movement while protecting my highest-weighted issues (A, B, and C), I will concede slightly on my lower-weighted issues (D, E, F) by proposing valueB for all three, which maintains a strong utility for me while showing a willingness to negotiate.

The opponent remains largely anchored to their highly advantageous opening, having made only a single minor concession on issueB while frequently employing repeated, scripted language framing their offers as a "win-win." As we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), I will maintain a firm stance on my most critical issues (A, B, and C). I will offer a newly structured concession by dropping issueF to valueC, seeking to explore if the opponent assigns higher priority to the lower-weight issues without compromising my core utility.

The current time fraction is very early (t=0.14), so I have no need to make rushed concessions. I will hold firm on my most heavily weighted issues (A, B, and C) which provide the highest utility. I notice the opponent switched from offering valueB on issueB to valueB on issueA, but both of these issues are highly critical to me, so I will continue to explore minor concessions on the lower-weighted issues D, E, and F to uncover what the opponent values.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.16), so I am maintaining a relatively high utility target (around 0.88). I noticed the opponent has recently shown flexibility on issueD (offering valueB in their last message) and previously on issueB (offering valueB). I will incorporate both issueD: valueB and issueB: valueB into my proposal while keeping my highest-weight issues (like issueA and issueC) at my most preferred values to gauge their response.

At t=0.18, it is still early in the negotiation, so I am targeting a high overall utility (above 0.85). The opponent has been experimenting with concessions on issues A, B, C, and D individually, but has strictly held on to "valueC" for both E and F. This suggests that E and F might be very important to them. Since E and F are among my lowest-weighted issues, I am offering a full concession on issue E to "valueC" and a partial concession on D to "valueB", while securing my highest priorities (A, B, C).

The opponent has made significant concessions on our mid-weight issueB by offering 'valueA', which I highly value, while maintaining their position on 'valueC' across other issues. At t=0.20, we have ample time, so I will lock in 'valueA' for issueB and hold firm on my highest priorities (issueA and issueC). To reciprocate their movement, I will make concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueD, issueE, and issueF), aiming to find an acceptable trade-off while retaining strong overall utility.

I am still in the early stages of the negotiation (t=0.22), so my priority is to maintain a high utility while exploring mutually beneficial trade-offs. The opponent has made a major concession on issueB by offering valueA—a high-weight issue for me—which signals flexibility. To reciprocate without sacrificing my top priorities (issueA, issueB, and issueC), I will maintain my preferred values on those while conceding on the lower-value issues (issueD, issueE, and issueF).

The opponent has systematically tested different issues and recently offered highly favorable values for my two most important issues, `issueA` and `issueB`. I will gladly anchor my next bid on `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueA` to signal agreement on these dimensions. To reciprocate their concessions, I will continue to offer flexible combinations of values on my lower-weight issues (D, E, F) to find a mutually acceptable tradeoff, while safely maintaining my overall utility above 0.80 since it is still early in the negotiation.

The opponent is systematically exploring different issues by making isolated concessions, most recently offering issueF: valueB. Since the time fraction is still early (t=0.26), I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to signal their importance. I will incorporate their recent concession on issueF and offer a slight concession on issueE to encourage further movement while keeping my overall utility above 0.84.

We are still early in the negotiation (t=0.28). The opponent's message history is highly repetitive and relies on generic claims of fairness, so I will rely entirely on their bidding behavior. They have previously conceded to `valueA` on issues A and B, suggesting those issues are lower priority for them, so I will hold firm there to secure my highest-weighted utility. I will concede to `valueC` on issues E and F, as they have shown extreme reluctance to move on them, while testing `valueB` on issue D to find a middle ground and keep my utility above 0.80.

The time fraction is still relatively early (t=0.3), so I will continue aiming for a utility of around 0.8. The opponent has previously shown a willingness to yield on issueA and issueB, as seen in their offers at t=0.24 and t=0.20, so I will firmly hold my highest-weighted issues (A, B, and C) at valueA. To find an agreeable compromise, I will test the waters by offering concessions on my lower-weighted issues (D, E, and F), ignoring the opponent's repetitive boilerplate messages claiming "win-win" balances.

The opponent has shown flexibility on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB), even proposing valueA for both independently in recent offers. They seem to be prioritizing issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF at valueC. Since the time fraction is still fairly early (t=0.32), I will maintain my top priorities (issueA, issueB, issueC at valueA) while continuing to explore acceptable tradeoffs by offering values closer to their preferences on my lower-weight issues (issueD, issueE, issueF).

I am ignoring the opponent's cheap-talk messages, which claim "major concessions" despite retracting previous better offers on priority issues like `issueA`. Since we are still early-mid game (t=0.34), I will stick to my high-priority issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, `issueC`) by holding them at their maximum utility for me (`valueA`). For the remaining lower-priority issues, I will explore new combinations involving `valueB` and `valueC` to test the opponent's hidden preferences.

The time fraction is 0.36, approaching the middle of the negotiation. The opponent has previously conceded on `issueA` and `issueB`, suggesting these hold lower importance for them, whereas they stubbornly hold on to `valueC` for `issueC`, `issueD`, `issueE`, and `issueF`. To find a win-win configuration, I will retain my highest-priority variables (`issueA`, `issueB`, `issueC`) at my top utility and offer a complete concession (`valueC`) on all my lower-weight issues (`issueD`, `issueE`, `issueF`). This targets a strong utility for me (0.76) while cleanly giving the opponent their preferred values on half the issues.

I am prioritizing my top-weighted issues (A, B, and C) while starting to make concessions on my lower-weighted issues (D, E, and F) since we are approaching the mid-game (t=0.38). The opponent has shown flexibility by offering `valueA` for issueB and `valueB` for issueE, so I am incorporating concessions on these less critical issues to signal cooperation while preserving my core utility.

The opponent has offered `issueE: valueA` in their latest bid, indicating they are willing to concede on this issue. I will incorporate this acceptable value while maintaining my firm stance on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`, which are my highest priorities. To keep the negotiation moving forward at t=0.40, I am offering concessions on my lower-weighted issues (`issueD` and `issueF`).

I am at `t=0.42` and will maintain strong utility on my top issues (A, B, C) while testing the waters for middle ground on the less critical ones. The opponent's last offer included `valueA` for `issueB` and `valueB` for `issueF`, matching my preferences on those fronts. I will accept those values and pair them with modest concessions on `issueD` and `issueE` to encourage further movement without risking my own utility goal.

The negotiation is approaching the mid-to-late phase (t=0.44). The opponent has frequently offered `valueC` for most issues, suggesting it holds high value for them, and they recently conceded on `issueD` with `valueA` and `issueC` with `valueB`. To capitalize on this without giving up my top three priorities (A, B, and C), I will maintain my request for `valueA` on those core issues while matching their recent move on `issueD` and granting them their heavily favored `valueC` on the lower-priority issues `issueE` and `issueF`.

The current time fraction is slightly before the halfway point (0.46), so I should maintain a firm stance on my most critical issues (A, B, and C) which collectively represent 70% of my total utility. The opponent's messages remain repetitive cheap talk about "win-win" concessions, but their actual offers fluctuate constantly, occasionally showing willingness to align with my preferred values (e.g., A=valueA at t=0.32, D=valueA at t=0.44). I am putting forward a new combination of partial concessions on my lower-weighted issues (E and F) to test where the opponent might find acceptable tradeoffs while still targeting a high utility close to 0.88.

The current time is 0.48, placing us near the midpoint of the negotiation. The opponent has made a major concession by offering my top value on my highest-priority issue (issueA: valueA) while attempting to lower my utility elsewhere. To progress the negotiation and acknowledge their concession on issueA, I will lock in issueA as valueA and maintain my top preferences on my next most important issues (B and C), while offering minor concessions on lower-priority issues (D, E, F) to present a mutually agreeable tradeoff.

At halfway through the allotted time, I will maintain high expectations for our most valuable items (issues A, B, and C) since the opponent has shown a willingness to yield on portions of my top demands in their recent offers (A:valueA at t=0.48 and B:valueA at t=0.50). The opponent's verbal messages claim consistent major concessions over time, but their bids bounce between conceding on issue A or issue B, so I can try to lock both down. I will concede further on the lowest-weight items (D, E, F) to present a realistic middle-ground while keeping my utility safely over 0.80.

The current time is 0.52, meaning we are now entering the second half of the negotiation. To reach an agreement, I will start making meaningful concessions on my side. I observe from your recent offers that you occasionally concede on `issueB` (offering `valueA`) and seem to prefer `valueB` for `issueC`. To match this, I will formally concede on `issueC` by shifting to `valueB` while also providing a favorable value on `issueF` (`valueC`), targeting an overall utility of approximately 0.84.

We are just past the halfway mark (t=0.54), and while the opponent's messages constantly claim their offers are "balanced" or "win-win", their actual proposals have hovered around 0.50 utility for me. However, their history shows willingness to offer `valueB` on multiple lower-weight issues (C, D, E, F) and even `valueA` occasionally on my high-priority issues (A and B). I will maintain my stance on my most critical issues, A and B, but concede to `valueB` on my lower-weight issues to inch closer to an agreement they can accept without giving up my core utility.

The negotiation has passed the midpoint (t=0.56). My opponent's assertions of fairness and minor fluctuations across issues suggest they may be testing my bottom line, though their previous offer at t=0.48 included `issueA: valueA` and `issueC: valueB`, making these realistic targets for agreement. I will propose a compromise that secures my highest priorities (`issueA: valueA`, `issueB: valueA`) and moderate utility on `issueC` while conceding on some lower-priority issues like `issueF` to find common ground.

The opponent's messages continually claim "win-win" and "major concessions," yet their offers fluctuate in a way that yields consistently low utility for me (around 0.44-0.48). Notably, they just proposed `issueE: valueA`, which aligns with my top preference for this issue. To capitalize on this, I will adopt `valueA` for `issueE` while holding firm on my most important issues, `issueA` and `issueB`. I will make a slight concession on the lower-weighted issues by offering `valueC` on `issueF` and `valueB` on `issueC` and `issueD`, aiming for an overall utility around 0.80 as we transition past the midpoint.

The opponent consistently offers `valueC` on most issues but has revealed flexibility by offering `valueA` on `issueB` and `issueF` recently. Since my weights for `issueD` and `issueE` are lowest (0.1), I will concede to their preferred `valueC` on these issues to show cooperation at t=0.6. I will hold firm on my most important issue, `issueA`, targeting a utility of 0.762 and using their recent concessions to find common ground.

The opponent's messaging contains generic "win-win" language, but their bidding behavior shows a consistent preference for `valueC` across all issues initially. They have recently shown willingness to concede to `valueA` on my most important issues (e.g., issueB and issueC). Thus, I will hold firm on getting `valueA` for `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`, maximizing my utility on the issues I weight most heavily (total importance 0.7), while making concessions on `issueD`, `issueE`, and `issueF` (which each hold only 0.1 importance to me) closer to their favored `valueC`.

Time is at 0.64, entering the late-mid game. Despite the opponent's continued assertions that their offers are perfectly balanced "win-win" scenarios, their bids tell me which concessions they are actually willing to make. Since they just offered valueA for issueC, valueB for issues D and E, and valueC for issueF, I will incorporate these exact terms into my offer while holding firm on my most important issues (A and B). This strategy allows me to align with their recent movement while targeting a high utility of roughly 0.84.

The negotiation is entering the mid-to-late game (t=0.66). The opponent's message uses generic language about finding a "balanced package," but their offer behavior continues to test different issues, including conceding `issueF` to `valueA` and softening on `issueB` and `issueE`. To preserve my high-weight issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, `issueC`) at top utility (`valueA`), I will meet them on their recent preferences by yielding on the lower-importance issues `issueD` and `issueE`, while holding `issueF` at `valueA` which they just offered themselves.

The opponent significantly conceded in their last offer by providing my top values on issueA, issueC, and issueD, although their utility for me was 0.68. Since time is at 0.68, I want to reciprocate while maintaining a high utility target of around 0.80. I will propose an offer that holds firm on my most important issues (A, B, and C) but makes major concessions on the lower weight issues (E and F) to align with the opponent's consistent demands for valueC on those issues.

The opponent's most recent offer (t=0.70) finally yielded on `issueA` with `valueA`, but sacrificed value on my other important issues (`issueB` and `issueC`), leaving the total utility around 0.56, which remains unacceptably low compared to my reservation value and current goals. However, noticing they incorporated `valueA` on `issueE` and `valueC` on `issueD`, I will concede slightly on those lower-weight issues (`issueD` and `issueF`) while securing my top three critical issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, `issueC`) at top utility. Given the t=0.70 mark, I aim for a utility around 0.84 to continue the narrowing process.

I am targeting a utility of around 0.80 to ensure my core interests are met while showing flexibility as we progress past the midpoint. The opponent's recent offer included valueA in issueF and valueB in issueE, which are low-weight for me; I will incorporate these into my offer to bridge the gap. In exchange, I am holding firm on my most critical issues (A and B) while slightly conceding on issueC and issueD.

We are in the late stages of negotiation (t=0.74) and I need to prioritize reaching an agreement while protecting my most critical interests. The opponent's recent offers show flexibility across different issues, and their message claims their latest offer is near-optimal. I will accept five of the six values from their exact most recent bid (issues B, C, D, E, and F) to signal strong alignment, but hold firm on my highest-priority issue (issueA) at valueA to maintain a utility of ~0.80 for myself.

The negotiation is entering its later stages (t=0.76), so I will continue making calculated concessions while protecting my top priorities (issues A and B). The opponent has shown sporadic willingness to accept `valueA` on issue A and combinations of other values, though their offers fluctuate widely. I will maintain `valueA` on my highest-priority issues (A and B) while offering a mix of tradeoffs in the lower-weight issues (C, D, E, F) to present a fresh offer that targets roughly 0.80 utility on my end.

The time in negotiation is progressing (t = 0.78), so I should slightly lower my utility target to the ~0.76 - 0.80 range. I am anchoring on issues A and B which are my most valuable issues, providing maximal utility. Looking at the opponent's history, they have occasionally conceded issues F and C, so I will propose an offer that balances some of my lower-priority issues (D and E) by giving them their preferred "valueC" and "valueB" while maintaining my core priorities.

The current time is 0.80, so I must start making more substantial concessions to secure an agreement before the deadline. While the opponent's messages constantly claim "major concessions" and "win-win", their offers still hover around 0.5 - 0.6 utility for me. To bridge the gap, I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB), but concede on my lower-priority ones (issueD, issueE, issueF) by offering values that the opponent has previously favored, bringing my utility down closer to a realistic compromise point (~0.72).
